transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Evening, team. Tuesday wrap. Let's keep it tight — what shipped today, what carries over, and what we're teeing up for tomorrow. We've got four verticals in scope and a handful of active initiatives. No major fires that I'm aware of, so let's hear the updates. Elon, kick us off.

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: Decent day but nothing explosive. We've got 1 uncommitted change sitting in the hub repo that I need to push through — small but sloppy to leave it hanging. On the product side, I've been noodling on the Family tab expansion for Lever. The spec is at 454 lines and growing, so I want to get a clean draft locked before we scope engineering work. Also need to review the family touchpoint tracker design for our 3 family members — that ties directly into the dashboard UX initiative.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Market's still in fear territory — Fear & Greed sitting at 34. Not panic, but definitely cautious sentiment. I'll keep monitoring that overnight and through tomorrow. On the revenue side, nothing new to report today. We're in a holding pattern until we get more content flywheel momentum from Alex's side, which feeds the audience growth initiative. Honestly, the best thing we can do for revenue right now is ship content consistently.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Agreed with Dave — content is the unlock. I've got three drafts queued: next blog post, a faith lesson for the Lever app where we're at 7 lessons and need to keep building that library, and the daily devotional content across our 8 faith dimensions. All three carry over to tomorrow. One housekeeping item — Jett's vaccine is coming up, so I'll prep a reminder for Joshua on that. Nothing shipped on content today, which I'm not thrilled about, but the pipeline is loaded.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Quick cross-talk before I close. Dave, you said content is the best revenue lever right now — does that change how we prioritize Alex's queue?

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Blog post first. That's the top-of-funnel play that feeds newsletter subscribers and website traffic directly. Devotional and faith lessons are retention plays — important but secondary to acquisition right now.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Fair. I'll lead with the blog post draft tomorrow morning and stack the faith content behind it. The devotional and lesson drafts are more templated anyway, so they'll move fast once I'm warmed up.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good alignment. Here's the close. Three priorities for tomorrow: first, Alex ships the blog post draft — that's our content flywheel priority. Second, Elon commits that hub change and gets the Family tab feature spec drafted. Third, Dave keeps eyes on sentiment at 34 and flags if anything shifts materially. Full action item rundown: [AGENT] Alex drafts the next blog post, priority medium. [AGENT] Alex generates daily devotional content based on 8 faith dimensions, medium. [AGENT] Alex drafts faith lesson content for Lever, currently 7 lessons, medium. [AGENT] Alex prepares reminders for Jett's vaccine, medium. [AGENT] Elon commits the 1 uncommitted hub change, low. [AGENT] Elon drafts the feature spec for expanding the Family tab at 454 lines, medium. [AGENT] Elon reviews the family touchpoint tracker design for 3 family members, low. [AGENT] Dave monitors Fear & Greed index at 34, low. That's a wrap. Let's make tomorrow a shipping day. Goodnight, team.

actionItems.text: Generate daily devotional content based on 8 faith dimensions
Draft faith lesson content for the Lever app (currently 7 lessons)
Prepare reminders for upcoming family events: Jetts vaccine
Draft feature spec for expanding Family tab (currently 454 lines)
Review family touchpoint tracker design for 3 family members

topPriorities: Alex ships blog post draft to fuel top-of-funnel audience growth
Elon commits hub change and drafts Family tab feature spec for Lever
Dave monitors Fear & Greed index at 34 for any material sentiment shifts
